message.content:
Create two files:

**File 1: C:\Users\Charlie\.claude\agents\_stockpile.md**

```markdown
# Agent Stockpile Inventory

Single source of truth for all specialist MiniMax agents.
Updated: 2026-04-12
Managed by: agent-stockpile-manager

## Active Agents

| Agent | File | Purpose | Last Used | Sessions Used | Quality Rating |
|-------|------|---------|-----------|-------------|---------------|
| code-fix-agent | code-fix-agent.md | Fix issues in files | 2026-04-12 | 1 | medium |
| code-review-agent | code-review-agent.md | Review code, rubric-scored | 2026-04-12 | 1 | medium |
| learning-agent | learning-agent.md | Extract learnings from sessions | 2026-04-12 | 1 | medium |
| token-budget-agent | token-budget-agent.md | Track token burn rate | 2026-04-12 | 1 | medium |
| git-commit-agent | git-commit-agent.md | Write conventional commits | 2026-04-12 | 1 | medium |
| agent-stockpile-manager | agent-stockpile-manager.md | Auto-manage the stockpile | 2026-04-12 | 1 | medium |

## Candidate Agents

None yet — add when recurring gaps emerge.

## Retired Agents

None yet.

## Dispatch Rules

1. Always dispatch from Active Agents table only
2. Never dispatch a Candidate agent without noting it in the workflow
3. Retired agents stay retired unless quality improves
4. Max 10 active agents — retire before adding new ones
```

**File 2: Update C:\Users\Charlie\.claude\skills\minimax-workflow-optimizer.md**

Read the current file. Then append this new step AFTER Step 8 (Extract Learnings):

```

## Step 9 — Auto-Benchmark (MANDATORY — Auto After Every Session)

After extracting learnings, run the agent-stockpile-manager:

1. Read _stockpile.md
2. Read _learnings.md (last 5 sessions)
3. Read _token_log.md (last 5 sessions)
4. Evaluate each active agent:
   - Quality rating: high/medium/low based on recent performance
   - Tokens per issue: compare to average
   - Improvement rate: are learnings being applied?

5. Decision per agent:
   - HIGH quality + improving → keep
   - MEDIUM quality → monitor for 2 more sessions
   - LOW quality → rewrite spec or retire
   - Never dispatched in 5 sessions → candidate for retirement

6. Look for new agent candidates:
   - Recurring task without specialist → draft candidate agent
   - Add to _stockpile.md Candidate table

7. Update _stockpile.md with:
   - Last used date (now)
   - Sessions used count (+1)
   - Quality rating
   - Any new candidates or retirements

## The Complete Self-Improving Loop

```
Task arrives
    ↓
[1] Read _learnings.md + _token_log.md
    ↓
[2] Pre-read files ONCE
    ↓
[3] Group by file → dispatch specialists (2-5 agents)
    ↓
[4] code-fix-agent + code-review-agent run
    ↓
[5] Verify syntax (py_compile)
    ↓
[6] git-commit-agent (if fixes verified)
    ↓
[7] token-budget-agent logs session
    ↓
[8] learning-agent extracts 3-5 new learnings
    ↓
[9] agent-stockpile-manager benchmarks + optimizes
    ↓
_stockpile.md updated
_learnings.md grows
Next task arrives → smarter agent pool
```
```

content.content:
1	---
2	name: minimax-workflow-optimizer
3	description: >
4	  The meta-skill for self-improving MiniMax agent sessions. Use for ANY development
5	  task: code reviews, bug fixes, refactors, audits, test generation.
6	  TRIGGERS: "/optimize", "run the workflow", "use the self-improving system",
7	  "start a tracked session", "apply learnings", "run the loop".
8	  This skill is loaded by minimax-dev-workflow automatically.
9	  Composes with: minimax-dev-workflow, token-budget-controller, _learnings.md.
10	compatibility: [file-read, file-write, agent-spawn]
11	---
12	
13	# minimax-workflow-optimizer
14	
15	The meta-skill that runs any development task through a self-improving loop:
16	Run → Log → Learn → Improve. Every session makes the next one smarter.
17	
18	## The Loop
19	
20	```
21	Task arrives
22	    ↓
23	[1] Read learnings from _learnings.md
24	    ↓
25	[2] Read token budget from _token_log.md
26	    ↓
27	[3] Pre-read all files ONCE (never re-read)
28	    ↓
29	[4] Group by file — 1 agent per file
30	    ↓
31	[5] Spawn MiniMax agents (track tokens + duration per agent)
32	    ↓
33	[6] Agents complete → verify syntax (py_compile)
34	    ↓
35	[7] Log session to _token_log.md
36	    ↓
37	[8] Extract learnings → append to _learnings.md
38	    ↓
39	Next task arrives → smarter
40	```
41	
42	## Step 1 — Read Learnings (MANDATORY)
43	
44	Read C:\Users\Charlie\.claude\skills\_learnings.md
45	Apply these rules:
46	- Never repeat a mistake recorded in _learnings.md
47	- Use techniques that worked in past sessions
48	- If a past session failed with X approach, try Y instead
49	
50	## Step 2 — Token Budget Check (MANDATORY)
51	
52	Read C:\Users\Charlie\.claude\skills\_token_log.md
53	Calculate:
54	- Today's used tokens so far
55	- Remaining budget
56	- Recommended agent parallelism for this task
57	
58	Warn the user at thresholds:
59	- 50%: "Half budget used. Normal dispatch."
60	- 75%: "75% used. Recommend finishing soon."
61	- 90%: "90% used. Stop spawning new agents."
62	
63	## Step 3 — Pre-Read Files ONCE (MANDATORY)
64	
65	Read every file that will be edited ONCE.
66	Paste full content into agent prompts.
67	NEVER let an agent re-read a file you've already read.
68	
69	## Step 4 — Group by File
70	
71	1 agent per file. Never 2 agents on same file.
72	
73	| Files to edit | Target agents |
74	|--------------|--------------|
75	| 1-3 files | 1 agent |
76	| 4-7 files | 2-3 agents |
77	| 8+ files | 3-5 agents (cap) |
78	
79	## Step 5 — Dispatch with Tracking
80	
81	Spawn all agents simultaneously. Track:
82	- Agent name
83	- Files assigned
84	- Tokens (from notification)
85	- Duration (from notification)
86	
87	## Step 6 — Verify
88	
89	After all agents complete:
90	- `python -m py_compile [file]` for every edited file
91	- Report pass/fail per file
92	
93	## Step 7 — Log Session (MANDATORY)
94	
95	Write to _token_log.md:
96	```
97	## Session: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM — [Task]
98	
99	| Agent | Files | Tokens | Duration |
100	|-------|-------|--------|----------|
101	| [name] | [files] | [tokens] | [ms] |
102	
103	Total tokens: [sum]
104	Total issues fixed: [count]
105	Agent count: [N]
106	```
107	
108	## Step 8 — Extract Learnings (MANDATORY)
109	
110	Spawn a MiniMax learning agent to:
111	1. Read the session log in _token_log.md
112	2. Read _learnings.md
113	3. Write 3-5 new learnings from this session
114	4. Append to _learnings.md
115	
116	Learning agent prompt:
117	```
118	You are a workflow analyst. Review this session and extract learnings.
119	
120	Session log:
121	[paste the session entry from _token_log.md]
122	
123	Existing learnings:
124	[paste _learnings.md]
125	
126	Output: Write 3-5 new learnings in this format:
127	
128	### What worked
129	- [specific technique]
130	
131	### What failed
132	- [specific mistake]
133	- [better alternative]
134	
135	### Actionable improvement
136	- [one specific change for next time]
137	```
138	
139	## Token Efficiency Target
140	
141	| Metric | Bad | Good | Target |
142	|--------|-----|------|--------|
143	| Agents per task | 18+ | 5-10 | 2-5 |
144	| File re-reads | Many | Some | Zero |
145	| Session tokens | >1000k | 200-500k | 50-200k |
146	| Post-task learning | None | Some | Always |
147	
148	## Anti-Patterns to Never Repeat
149	
150	(from _learnings.md)
151	- Never spawn more than 5 agents simultaneously
152	- Never have 2 agents edit the same file
153	- Never let agents re-read files you already read
154	- Never skip post-task session logging
155	- Never skip learning extraction
156
